preview_text:
The model favors Spain at 41%, with Argentina trailing at 28% — a 13-percentage-point gap. Spain emerges as the modest favorite in this neutral-venue encounter, though the margin leaves Argentina with a realistic path to victory.

The draw probability of 31% sits roughly a third of the match probability space, indicating a competitive fixture unlikely to be decided by a large margin. The win probabilities are not dramatically separated, suggesting Spain's edge is real but not commanding. A stalemate after 90 minutes remains a plausible outcome.

Model and market align closely across all three outcomes. The sportsbook consensus sits within 1 percentage point of the model on each line, while Polymarket prices are similarly tight. This convergence suggests broad agreement on the match dynamics, with no material divergence between the statistical forecast and betting consensus to highlight.
